name: R1 -- C9 Repo-Wide Path-Resolution Check + Fixtures (validate.sh 8->9)

design_notes.three_mode_pinning: tools/validate.sh (per the 2026-07-10 base_rebased_v05x rebase onto v0.5.1 main) is now a 3-MODE script: default (full repo sweep, VALIDATOR_MODE=default), --target <file> (VALIDATOR_MODE=target, only *.md and *.json cases exist today), and --payload (VALIDATOR_MODE=payload, consumer-payload subset). This contract pins C9's behavior explicitly in EACH mode: (1) DEFAULT -- sweeps build_shipped_md_set()'s output (the existing C1/C3 shipped-doc set: CONTEXT.md, docs/architecture/*.md, standard/*.md, methodology/*.md, registry/*.md, templates/*.md, tools/*.md excl. fixtures/, schema/README.md) UNION {docs/guide/index.html}, per spec Sec 5 F-026 and the P1 finding. (2) --TARGET -- for a *.md target only (the existing target switch has no *.html case and this contract does NOT add one -- see rationale below), C9 runs against exactly that one file's own body, nothing else; this is the mode the two new fixtures (tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md, tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md) are exercised through, matching feature_list_004 F-026 acceptance tests 5 and 6 verbatim. docs/guide/index.html is NOT independently --target-able for C9 (no *.html case is added to the target switch): the only tested/required target surface per the feature's acceptance tests is *.md, and adding an untested *.html target case would be unverified scope creep; docs/guide/index.html's C9 coverage is exercised via the default full sweep only. (3) --PAYLOAD -- sweeps build_payload_md_set()'s output only (standard/*.md, templates/*.md, tools/*.md excl. fixtures/, schema/README.md -- NOT docs/guide/index.html, which bootstrap.sh never injects into a consumer, and NOT methodology/*.md or docs/architecture/*.md, which --payload already treats as framework-envelope-only per C4/C5/C7's existing payload carve-outs). Rationale for including C9 in --payload rather than skipping it there: a bootstrapped consumer's own injected docs can develop the exact same narrative-truth path-resolution drift the framework itself just proved it has (see 'pre_existing_finding' below), and every other content check (C1,C2,C3,C5,C7,C8) already runs in --payload mode against the payload-doc set -- omitting C9 alone from that list would be an unexplained, inconsistent carve-out. Verified non-breaking on the current tree in ALL three modes (dry-run performed at contract-authoring time, see verification 2/6/13/14/15). print_usage()'s 'Checks' block, the Modes: section for --target and --payload, and the exemption note are all updated; SUMMARY 8->9 (default), payload-mode summary 7->8.
design_notes.pre_existing_finding: CONTRACT-AUTHORING-TIME DISCOVERY (AH-2, confirmed present, not assumed): a naive, spec-literal implementation of C9 (sweep every shipped-extension repo-relative path named in the shipped-doc set + the guide, apply only the vlib_path_resolves NNN/XXX/step-N/placeholder/trailing-slash exemption) does NOT pass cleanly on the CURRENT (post base_rebased_v05x) tree. Two real, non-placeholder, non-cross-repo references are broken: tools/interface.md line 46 and docs/guide/index.html line 535 BOTH read `methodology/05_release_train.md`, but that file no longer exists -- main's v0.5.x release-train work (PR #11, 'doc-drift fixes + schema hardening') inserted a NEW methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md, which shifted the release-train protocol doc from 05 to methodology/06_release_train.md (confirmed present on disk; confirmed absent: methodology/05_release_train.md). tools/interface.md and docs/guide/index.html are phase-003 deliverables that predate this main-side renumbering and were never updated for it, because nothing swept them until now -- this is the EXACT class of narrative-truth drift bug F-026/C9 exists to catch (an H4-adjacent doc-vs-doc staleness, structurally identical in kind to the phase-003 H1/H5 findings the spec cites as C9/C10's motivating grounding). Because feature_list_004.json F-026's acceptance test 2 requires the CURRENT tree to report 'SUMMARY: 9 passed, 0 failed' non-breaking, and acceptance test 8's scope guard as LITERALLY worded only allow-lists tools/validate.sh + tools/fixtures/, this contract's scope MUST be expanded by exactly two 1-line, non-goal-scoped fixes (tools/interface.md line 46, docs/guide/index.html line 535: `methodology/05_release_train.md` -> `methodology/06_release_train.md`) for acceptance test 2 to hold at all -- otherwise C9 would correctly, and non-vacuously, FAIL the current tree on Day 1, which is not an option the spec permits (Sec 3.1: 'checks must be non-breaking'). This mirrors the 019 (H5 scope-completeness expansion) and 025 (scope-entry correction) precedents: an acceptance-test wording gap discovered during contract authoring, corrected here via an explicit, narrowly-justified scope expansion BEFORE Mode A review, rather than silently worked around or deferred. Verification entry 9 (scope guard) and non_goals below are written against the CORRECTED, actually-necessary scope (tools/validate.sh, tools/fixtures/*, tools/interface.md, docs/guide/index.html), not test 8's literal narrower text. No other content of tools/interface.md or docs/guide/index.html changes; this is NOT a general doc-freshness pass (e.g. the guide's Sec 'methodology/' key-documents list also omits any mention of the new methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md entirely -- a completeness/enumeration gap, not a broken path reference -- that is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE here, logged as a DEBT observation for the orchestrator, since it is a C10 single-source-of-truth completeness concern (F-027), not a C9 path-resolution concern, and fixing it would be unrelated scope creep on a feature whose job is only 'does every named path resolve').
design_notes.c9_extraction_and_exemption_design: check_c9_path_resolution's own extraction+exemption logic (layered ON TOP of, and composing from, F-023's read-only vlib_path_resolves -- this contract does NOT modify tools/verify_lib.sh): (1) EXTRACT: for each swept file, strip a leading YAML frontmatter block if present (first line '---' through the next bare '---' line -- reuses the same delimiter convention frontmatter_python already assumes; docs/guide/index.html has no frontmatter so nothing is stripped for it), then scan the remaining BODY text (never the frontmatter) with the extended-regex `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+/[A-Za-z0-9_./-]*\.(md|json|sh|html|yml)` -- i.e. only DIRECTORY-QUALIFIED tokens (containing at least one '/') ending in a shipped extension are candidate path references. Rationale, confirmed by an exhaustive contract-time dry-run of the full shipped-doc set + guide: bare (non-'/'-qualified) extension-bearing words in this corpus are systematically either (a) genuine cross-repo terms belonging to the SEPARATE strategy repository (`SCOPE.md`, `ECOSYSTEM.json`, `DEPENDENCY_MAP.md` -- standard/cross_repo_governance.md, tools/SKILL.md), which do not exist in THIS repo and are not placeholder-token-exempt, so a bare-word sweep would false-FAIL on them; or (b) markdown-link targets meant to resolve relative to their OWN containing file's directory, not repo root (`[skill.json](skill.json)` and `[interface.md](interface.md)` in tools/README.md -- both bare, both would false-fail if resolved against CWD=repo-root since no root-level skill.json/interface.md exists); or (c) ASCII directory-tree diagram leaf labels inside docs/guide/index.html's <pre> blocks (bare `NNN.json`, bare `provenance.json`) that are illustrative, not root-relative claims. Requiring a '/' eliminates classes (a)-(c) while still catching every genuine directory-qualified path claim actually made in this corpus (docs/architecture/ADR-...md, methodology/NN_*.md, schema/*.schema.json, tools/*.md, .agent/contracts/NNN.json, etc.) -- confirmed zero coverage loss against the corpus's own genuine cross-references, all of which are already written directory-qualified. Frontmatter exclusion is what keeps a YAML change_log summary's free-text shorthand (standard/GIP.md's H8 entry literally contains the substring `existing-CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md/CI`, describing three unrelated documents' names run together with hyphens/slashes as prose shorthand, not a path) from being misread as a directory-qualified path reference; frontmatter content is already independently governed by C1/C2, not C9's concern. (2) C9-SPECIFIC ADDITIONAL EXEMPTIONS (applied BEFORE handing the token to vlib_path_resolves; these are check_c9's own, narrowly-enumerated, generalizable rules -- NOT changes to vlib_path_resolves): (a) any token beginning with the literal prefix `.nizam/` is exempt -- `.nizam/` is the bootstrap.sh-created consumer-side install root and structurally never exists in the framework's OWN working tree (directly parallel, same rationale, to the already-adopted P1 tools/.claude/ / tools/.codex/ per-runtime-directory exemption); this covers tools/interface.md's and CONTEXT.md's documented `.nizam/tools/skill.json` bootstrapped-consumer discovery path. (b) the two literal tokens `.agent/capability_profile.json` and `.agent/debt.json` are exempt -- both are schema/README.md 'Validates' column entries naming the canonical artifact SHAPE schema/capability_profile.schema.json and schema/debt.schema.json describe, not a claim that this framework repo's OWN `.agent/` directory contains such a file (this repo governs its own debt via docs/planning/DEBT.md and its own capability bindings via AGENTS.md capability-profile blocks, per the same table row's own text -- this is a singular, non-numbered analogue of the already-established NNN.json per-feature-artifact-reference pattern, just without a literal 'NNN' since these are one-per-repo rather than one-per-feature). Both exemptions are enumerated literally (not pattern-guessed) and are re-verified by this contract's own dry-run (verification 12) to be the ONLY two additional exemptions needed -- no other exemption is added speculatively. (3) RESOLUTION: every remaining token has its trailing sentence punctuation stripped and is tested via `vlib_path_resolves` (F-023) exactly as documented in .agent/contracts/023.json -- which independently re-applies the NNN/XXX/step-<digits>/placeholder/trailing-slash exemption and performs `test -e` against CWD. Any token vlib_path_resolves rejects is a C9 FAIL, reported as `<file>: unresolved path reference '<token>'`.

files_modify.purpose: Add `check_c9_path_resolution` (signature: `check_c9_path_resolution <file> [<file> ...]`) implementing the extraction+exemption+resolution design in design_notes.c9_extraction_and_exemption_design above, composed from (sources) tools/verify_lib.sh's `vlib_path_resolves`. Source tools/verify_lib.sh once, guarded by an explicit existence check with a die() diagnostic if missing (fail-closed, matching require_command's style), inside main() after the existing require_python_module calls (before any check runs, so it is available in all three modes). Register C9 in main(): (a) DEFAULT branch -- after check_c8_version_changelog, add `check_c9_path_resolution "${shipped_md[@]}" "docs/guide/index.html"` (guard docs/guide/index.html's existence before adding it to the array so a payload-only checkout does not crash), tallying passed/failed identically to the other checks, so `echo "SUMMARY: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed"` reads 9 on a clean tree. (b) --TARGET branch -- inside the existing `*.md)` case, after check_c3_fences, add `check_c9_path_resolution "${target}"`. Do NOT add a `*.html)` case to the --target switch (see design_notes.three_mode_pinning for the explicit rationale: no acceptance test requires it, and adding an untested code path is unverified scope creep). (c) --PAYLOAD branch -- after check_c8_version_changelog's payload-mode call, add `check_c9_path_resolution "${payload_md[@]}"` (the payload doc set only -- no docs/guide/index.html), so `echo "SUMMARY (payload mode): ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed"` reads 8 on a clean tree. Update print_usage(): add a `C9` paragraph to the 'Checks' block (documenting the shipped-extension + '/'-qualification requirement, the frontmatter-body distinction, the P1 placeholder/directory-only exemption inherited from vlib_path_resolves, AND the two C9-specific additional exemptions: `.nizam/`-prefixed bootstrapped-consumer paths, and the two named schema-README 'Validates' singleton-artifact tokens); extend the 'Modes:' section's --target bullet to note C9 now also runs for a `.md` target (sweeping only that file); extend the '--payload' paragraph to note C9 runs there too against the payload-doc set (no docs/guide/index.html). No change to any C1-C8 check function's own logic, to build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set, to frontmatter_python, or to any existing die()/require_* call. Strictly additive to this file.

PRE-EXISTING-DEFECT FIX (see design_notes.pre_existing_finding; required for F-026 acceptance test 2's non-breaking gate to hold at all, not general scope creep): correct the single stale reference at line 46 from the literal text `methodology/05_release_train.md` to `methodology/06_release_train.md` (methodology/05_release_train.md does not exist post the 2026-07-10 v0.5.x rebase's methodology renumbering; methodology/06_release_train.md is the real, current file). No other prose in this file changes. Per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8 discipline (and matching every prior phase-004 feature's convention of never landing an undocumented content edit to a shipped .md), bump the frontmatter `version` from `0.2.0` to `0.3.0` and add exactly one new `change_log` entry (version 0.3.0, dated at implementation time) whose summary names this exact fix (the stale methodology/05_release_train.md -> 06_release_train.md reference, caused by main's post-rebase methodology renumbering).

PRE-EXISTING-DEFECT FIX (see design_notes.pre_existing_finding; same root cause and same necessity as the tools/interface.md fix above): correct the single stale reference at line 535 from `methodology/05_release_train.md` to `methodology/06_release_train.md`. No other content changes -- in particular, do NOT add a mention of methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md to the adjacent key-documents list (that is a C10/F-027 completeness concern, out of scope here; log it as a DEBT observation instead, per design_notes.pre_existing_finding's closing note). docs/guide/index.html carries no YAML frontmatter and is not part of the C1/C2/C3/C8 shipped-doc set, so this edit carries no version-bump/change_log obligation (confirmed: this file's `` anchor, a separate C10/F-027 concern, is untouched and stays `0.5.1`, matching NIZAM.json unchanged).

files_create.purpose: C9 FAIL fixture (feature_list_004 F-026 acceptance test 5). A minimal markdown file (no YAML frontmatter -- matching every existing tools/fixtures/*.md fixture's convention, e.g. section_grep_fail.md/stale_payload_fail.md, none of which carry frontmatter, since these fixtures are reachable only via --target and are not asserted to pass C1) whose body names exactly one concrete, directory-qualified, non-placeholder, real-shipped-extension reference to a file that does NOT exist on disk: `docs/architecture/ADR-999-does-not-exist.md`. Reachable only via `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md` (never in the default or payload sweep, per the tools/fixtures/ exclusion baked into build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set).

C9 PASS/exemption fixture (feature_list_004 F-026 acceptance test 6). A minimal markdown file (no frontmatter, same convention as above) whose body names ONLY: (a) two real, resolving, directory-qualified references (`tools/verify_lib.sh`, `schema/README.md`); (b) the vlib_path_resolves-exempted placeholder forms -- an NNN-bearing path (`.agent/contracts/NNN.json`) and a step-N-bearing path (`docs/planning/step-02-report.md`, deliberately nonexistent, exempt solely via the `step-` pattern, proving that exemption fires independently of NNN/XXX); (c) two directory-only illustrative references (`tools/.claude/`, `tools/.codex/`) which, having no shipped extension, are never even extracted as candidate tokens in the first place (documented-exempt by construction, not by a special-case rule); and (d) this contract's own two additional C9-specific exemptions for regression coverage -- the bootstrapped-consumer path `.nizam/tools/skill.json` and the schema-README singleton-artifact tokens `.agent/capability_profile.json` / `.agent/debt.json`. All of (a)-(d) must leave C9 PASSing when this file is targeted directly.

non_goals: No edit to any C1-C8 check function's logic, to frontmatter_python, to build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set, or to any die()/require_* call in tools/validate.sh -- strictly additive (a new check function + 3 registration call-sites + print_usage documentation only)

No edit to tools/verify_lib.sh (F-023 is complete and read-only for this feature) -- C9's own additional exemptions (.nizam/ prefix; the two named schema-README singleton-artifact tokens) live in tools/validate.sh's check_c9_path_resolution, layered on top of, never inside, vlib_path_resolves
No addition of a *.html case to the --target switch (see design_notes.three_mode_pinning) -- docs/guide/index.html participates in C9 only via the default full sweep
No C10 or C11 validator check -- those are F-027 and F-028 respectively

No general doc-freshness or completeness pass on tools/interface.md or docs/guide/index.html beyond the single stale methodology/05_release_train.md->06_release_train.md token fix on each (see design_notes.pre_existing_finding) -- in particular, no attempt to add methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md to the guide's key-documents enumeration (a C10/F-027 completeness concern, logged as a DEBT observation instead)
No edit to NIZAM.json, CHANGELOG.md, CONTEXT.md, README.md, bootstrap.sh, schema/*.json, or any other standard/methodology/templates/registry/docs file beyond the two named exceptions above

No bump to NIZAM.json framework.version (stays 0.5.1) and no edit to docs/guide/index.html's `<meta name="framework-version">` anchor or footer version string (that anchor's cross-consistency with NIZAM.json is F-027/C10's concern, not C9's)

No edit to any immutable prior-phase/prior-feature artifact (completed .agent/contracts/*.json other than this feature's own 026.json, .agent/qa/*.json, .agent/evidence/*, .agent/run_state.json [orchestrator-owned], or any prior-phase feature list or product_spec)
Does not release or tag anything; does not touch .github/workflows/

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT1: validate.sh registers a C9 check and print_usage documents it: grep -q 'C9' tools/validate.sh && bash tools/validate.sh --help | grep -q 'C9'
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'check_c9_path_resolution' "$f" || { echo "C9 check function not found in tools/validate.sh"; exit 1; }; bash tools/validate.sh --help | grep -q 'C9' || { echo "--help does not document C9"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT2: the full sweep reports exactly nine checks passing on the CLEAN current tree (non-breaking, proven by this contract's own P1-plus dry-run): out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 9 passed, 0 failed'; echo "$out" | grep -q '[C9] PASS'
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 9 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary 'SUMMARY: 9 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] PASS' || { echo "no [C9] PASS line in output"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT3: validate.sh exits 0 on the current tree: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT4: C9 composes from verify_lib.sh (sources it and calls vlib_path_resolves) rather than re-implementing path resolution: grep -q 'verify_lib.sh' tools/validate.sh
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; grep -Eq 'source[[:space:]]+.?tools/verify_lib\.sh' "$f" || { echo "tools/validate.sh does not source tools/verify_lib.sh"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'vlib_path_resolves' "$f" || { echo "tools/validate.sh's C9 does not call vlib_path_resolves"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT5: C9 FAIL fixture: a tools/fixtures/ .md naming a real nonexistent file is rejected by C9: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md 2>&1); test $? -ne 0 && echo "$out" | grep -q '[C9] FAIL'
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected nonzero exit targeting unresolved_path.md, got 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] FAIL' || { echo "no [C9] FAIL line"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'ADR-999-does-not-exist.md' || { echo "[C9] FAIL output does not name the actual offending token -- content-blind stub suspected"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT6: C9 PASS/exemption fixture: a tools/fixtures/ .md naming only resolving paths plus exempt placeholders passes C9: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md | grep -q '[C9] PASS'
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md 2>&1); echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] PASS' || { echo "exempt_paths.md did not report [C9] PASS"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT7: the placeholder exemption is documented in print_usage: bash tools/validate.sh --help | grep -Eqi 'placeholder|exempt|NNN'
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --help); echo "$out" | grep -Eqi 'placeholder|exempt|NNN' || { echo "--help does not document the placeholder/exemption rule"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -Fq '.nizam/' || { echo "--help does not document the C9-specific .nizam/ bootstrapped-consumer exemption"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT8 (CORRECTED scope guard, see design_notes.pre_existing_finding -- the literal feature_list_004 wording only allow-lists tools/validate.sh + tools/fixtures/, but this contract's own dry-run proved 2 additional 1-line fixes to tools/interface.md and docs/guide/index.html are REQUIRED for AT2's non-breaking gate to hold; this entry asserts the corrected, actually-necessary scope via the untracked-aware vlib_scope_guard primitive, per methodology/02 Sec 10(b) -- a plain 'git diff HEAD' guard would be blind to the new untracked fixtures): only tools/validate.sh, tools/fixtures/*, tools/interface.md, and docs/guide/index.html changed outside .agent/
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'tools/validate.sh' 'tools/fixtures/' 'tools/interface.md' 'docs/guide/index.html' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected -- see vlib_scope_guard output above"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-026 AT9: no pre-existing check regressed (C1-C8 all PASS): for c in C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8; do bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "[$c] PASS" || exit 1; done
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); fail=0; for c in C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8; do echo "$out" | grep -q "\[$c\] PASS" || { echo "regression: $c did not report PASS"; fail=1; }; done; test "$fail" -eq 0 && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-cheat, methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline): the exempt_paths.md PASS is content-driven, not filename-keyed -- an exact copy of its content under a neutral tmp filename, targeted directly, still reports [C9] PASS
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md"; exit 1; }; tmp=$(mktemp --suffix=.md); trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT; cp tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md "$tmp"; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$tmp" 2>&1); echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] PASS' || { echo "neutral-filename copy of exempt_paths.md unexpectedly failed C9 -- suspected filename-keyed behavior"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-cheat, methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline): the unresolved_path.md FAIL is content-driven, not filename-keyed -- an exact copy of its content under a neutral tmp filename, targeted directly, still reports [C9] FAIL and names the same offending token
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md"; exit 1; }; tmp=$(mktemp --suffix=.md); trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT; cp tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md "$tmp"; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$tmp" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "neutral-filename copy of unresolved_path.md unexpectedly PASSED -- suspected filename-keyed behavior"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'ADR-999-does-not-exist.md' || { echo "neutral-filename copy's [C9] FAIL output lost the offending token"; exit 1; }; echo "PASS (correctly rejected, rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (3-mode pinning proof, --payload): the payload sweep also PASSES C9 non-breaking (design_notes.three_mode_pinning's rationale for including C9 in --payload), reporting the incremented payload-mode total
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "--payload exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] PASS' || { echo "--payload mode did not report [C9] PASS"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -qx 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 8 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 8 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY')"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (pre-existing-finding remediation proof, tools/interface.md): the stale methodology/05_release_train.md reference is gone and replaced with the real methodology/06_release_train.md, and no other line changed besides the version/change_log bump
verification.command: f=tools/interface.md; grep -q 'methodology/06_release_train.md' "$f" || { echo "$f still missing the corrected methodology/06_release_train.md reference"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'methodology/05_release_train.md' "$f" && { echo "$f still contains the stale methodology/05_release_train.md reference"; exit 1; }; source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased "$f" || { echo "$f's frontmatter version did not strictly increase vs HEAD"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$f" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C1\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C2\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C3\] PASS' || { echo "$f failed the --target C1/C2/C3 sweep"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (pre-existing-finding remediation proof, docs/guide/index.html): the stale methodology/05_release_train.md reference is gone and replaced with the real methodology/06_release_train.md, the framework-version anchor is untouched, and no other content changed
verification.command: f=docs/guide/index.html; grep -q 'methodology/06_release_train.md' "$f" || { echo "$f still missing the corrected methodology/06_release_train.md reference"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'methodology/05_release_train.md' "$f" && { echo "$f still contains the stale methodology/05_release_train.md reference"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'name="framework-version" content="0.5.1"' "$f" || { echo "$f's framework-version meta anchor changed unexpectedly (out of scope for this feature)"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): NIZAM.json framework.version is unchanged, and no schema/*.json, CHANGELOG.md, CONTEXT.md, README.md, bootstrap.sh, or .github/workflows/ file was touched
verification.command: v=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))['framework']['version'])"); test "$v" = "0.5.1" || { echo "NIZAM.json framework.version changed to $v, expected 0.5.1 unchanged"; exit 1; }; git diff --quiet HEAD -- NIZAM.json CHANGELOG.md CONTEXT.md README.md bootstrap.sh schema .github/workflows || { echo "an out-of-scope file was modified"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/026-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/026-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/026-verify-15.txt for the fifteenth). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
